title: sorghum timorense
intro: Sorghum timorense is a plant species in the sorghum genus, native to the island of Timor, and is known for its medicinal properties. The plant contains various bioactive compounds, including flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which contribute to its health benefits. Traditionally, Sorghum timorense is used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties. In herbal preparations, the plant can be consumed as a tea or used in infusions, and is also sometimes incorporated into traditional remedies in the form of decoctions or tinctures.
